mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months signals rising inflation expectations, leading to higher nominal interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5-day forward returns in rising inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the Unemployment Rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wage growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in Industrial Production over the past 6 months signals rising economic activity, leading to higher demand for raw materials and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in rising economic activity periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in Housing Starts over the past 3 months signals rising housing demand, leading to higher construction activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in rising housing demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the Federal Funds Rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to higher borrowing costs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 20-day forward returns in tightening monetary cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in Nonfarm Payrolls over the past 3 months signals rising labor demand, leading to higher wage growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only when the Unemployment Rate is below 5%.
regime_hint: tightening labor market with low unemployment
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods with low unemployment are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in Real Gross Domestic Product (GDP) over the past 6 months signals rising economic activity, leading to higher demand for goods and services and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only during the second half of the year.
regime_hint: rising economic activity in the second half of the year
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising economic activity periods in the second half of the year are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
